description: Join adjacent blocks in the range. Adjacent blocks within an optional range can be joined into one block ( on ) or or unjoined ( off ). Joined blocks act as one block with a common centroid and volume calculated for all blocks joined together. A joined block group consists of one “leader” block and one or more “follower” blocks. Use the block list information command to identify leader and follower blocks in a block group. Note: only visible blocks are joined (see block hide ).
